commit.message:
Fix alternation leftmost-match position bug (#151)

* Add comprehensive tests for alternation leftmost-match position bug

Tests for issue #150: SafeRE returns wrong match positions when
alternation alternatives can match at different starting positions.

Adds AlternationLeftmostPositionTest with 97 tests (19 currently
failing) covering:

- Basic different-position alternation (short/long text)
- Issue #150 exact regression patterns (generics, API version)
- Capturing vs non-capturing groups
- Multiple alternatives, nested alternation
- Quantifiers, character classes, dot wildcards
- Long text (DFA path) with same vs different match endpoints
- Minimal reproductions isolating shared-prefix partial-match behavior
- Exhaustive cross-engine tests with multi-char alternation atoms
- Real-world patterns (HTTP methods, version strings, paths)

The bug manifests in two dimensions:
1. DFA path (long text): fails when alternatives have different endpoints
2. Short text: fails when alt1 partially matches before failing while
   alt2 fully matches at the same position

Refs #150

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* AGENTS.md: clarify test-first workflow for bug fixing

Emphasize that test coverage must come before investigating engine
internals. The workflow is: identify the class of bugs, write systematic
tests, run them, then investigate and fix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix alternation leftmost-match bug by skipping DFA sandwich

The DFA sandwich (forward DFA → reverse DFA → anchored forward DFA)
incorrectly identifies the match start for alternation patterns when
alternatives match at different positions with different endpoints.
The forward DFA with longest=false returns the earliest match END,
which can come from a later-starting alternative that ends sooner,
causing the reverse DFA to miss the leftmost-starting match.

For example, (bcd|abcde) on text containing 'abcde': the forward DFA
returns end=4 from the 'bcd' match (starting at 1), missing the
leftmost 'abcde' match starting at 0 (which ends at 5).

Fix: mark dfaStartReliable() as false for alternation patterns, so
the DFA sandwich is skipped and the BitState/NFA engine handles the
search. The forward DFA existence check still runs for the fast
negative path.

Fixes #150

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
add read_all_pvts: parse all NAV-PVTs from each FIFO drain

Previously read_pvt() drained up to 512 bytes from the FIFO and returned
only the first NAV-PVT found, silently discarding the rest (up to 4 more
per 512-byte read at 100 bytes/PVT). This caused significant sample loss
whenever the FIFO accumulated multiple packets — e.g. during a 16-second
Notecard transmission at 25 Hz GPS: 40 PVTs queued in chip FIFO, but only
~8 were extracted (1 per 512-byte drain × 8 reads).

Changes:
- Refactor parse_nav_pvt into iter_nav_pvts(buf, &mut f) that calls f()
  for EVERY valid NAV-PVT found in the buffer.  parse_nav_pvt is now a
  thin wrapper (behaviorally unchanged).
- Add MaxM10S::read_all_pvts(i2c, &mut f): drains one chunk and invokes
  f for every packet.  Returns Ok(true) if FIFO had data, Ok(false) when
  empty.  Caller loops until false to fully drain the FIFO.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(plan-db): task locking + wave PR dedup for concurrent agents

TaskLockGate: prevents two agents from claiming the same task.
When agent A sets task to in_progress, locked_by is set to A.
If agent B tries to claim the same task, the gate returns 409
with the name of the locking agent. Lock clears on submit/done/fail.

WavePrDedupGate: enforces one-PR-per-wave rule at the gate level.
If task 1 in wave W0 has PR #626 in notes and task 2 tries to
submit with PR #627, the gate blocks with a clear error pointing
to the existing PR URL.

Schema: migration v24 adds locked_by column to tasks table.

Root cause: Plan 1127 spawned parallel agents that both claimed
the same tasks, creating 4 duplicate PRs (#623-#627).

Closes #649

* fix: FASTA file count rule now respects fasta_policy

The FASTA requirement depends on experiment type, not a blanket mandate:
- metaproteomics/proteogenomics → error (must upload FASTA)
- standard public DB → warning (provide name/version in metadata instead)
- patient-specific DB → warning (privacy exemption)

Changed all 10 tool profiles' fasta_file_count rules from severity: error
to severity: warning with conditional_severity overrides for metaproteomics
and proteogenomics flags.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: FASTA is recommended (not mandatory) for proteogenomics

Proteogenomics databases may contain controlled-access or patient-specific
genomic data that cannot be shared. Changed from mandatory to recommended
across fasta_policy, conditional_override, and all cross_file_rules.
Metaproteomics remains mandatory (error).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: missing columns are warnings, empty files remain errors

Column names may vary across tool versions (e.g. DIA-NN, MaxQuant), so a
missing column should warn, not block submission. But a file with zero
data rows means something is fundamentally wrong — that stays as error.

commit.message:
fix: uptime monitor — dual-schedule + 90s timeout + retry on cold-start

Problems with previous monitor:
- Single cron */5 can be delayed 10-30+ min by GitHub — Render spins down in 15min
- 45s max-time expired before Render cold-start finished (takes 50-90s)
- On 000 response, gave up with no retry

Fixes:
- TWO overlapping schedules: */5 (at :00,:05..) and 2-59/5 (at :02,:07..)
  Together fire every ~2-3 min — both would need delay simultaneously to miss
- 90s max-time handles full Render cold-start window
- On 000: wait 90s then ping again to confirm wake + reset idle timer
- On 503: wait 30s then ping again
- Single job pings both sequentially (saves GitHub Actions runner quota)

commit.message:
feat(concourse): cross-env GitHub issue gate for split Concourse pipelines

When a pipeline is split across two Concourse environments (e.g. QA stack in QA
Concourse, Production stack in prod Concourse) the normal intra-chain GitHub issue
promotion gate is broken: pulumi_jobs_chain only creates the trigger resource when
there is a next stage in the same chain.

Changes:
- Add prior_stage_stack field to SimplePulumiParams: when set, build_simple_pulumi_pipeline
  creates a github_issues trigger resource for that stack and injects it as a
  custom_dependencies[0] entry so the first job in the new chain gates on the prior
  stage's deployment issue being closed.
- Set prior_stage_stack="substructure.starrocks.lakehouse.QA" on the
  starrocks-substructure (Production-only) entry so the production deployment in prod
  Concourse waits for the QA deployment issue that was posted by QA Concourse.
- Fix mypy type annotation for extra_args in meta.py __main__ block.

Merge hotreload-utils source into runtime repo (#126512)

Copy the hotreload-utils source code from dotnet/hotreload-utils into
src/tools/hotreload-delta-gen/ and replace the NuGet PackageReference
with direct in-repo MSBuild targets, eliminating the cross-repo
dependency.

Changes:
- Add 5 projects + shared code from hotreload-utils under
src/tools/hotreload-delta-gen/
- Create eng/testing/hotreload-delta-gen.targets replacing the NuGet
package's .targets file
- Update 3 consumers (ApplyUpdate tests, WASM HotReload test, Mono
sample) to import local targets instead of NuGet package
- Add BuildTool and Tasks to src/libraries/pretest.proj for build
ordering
- Remove hotreload-utils dependency from eng/Version.Details.xml and
eng/Version.Details.props
- Suppress analyzers on imported code (RunAnalyzers=false) matching the
illink pattern

---------
